channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Before the Pac-12 disappears: Our picks for each school’s football Mt. Rushmore (players and coaches)
item.description: We selected four players and one coach from each of the 12 schools for their hypothetical football Mt. Rushmore.
item.dc:creator: Jon Wilner, The Mercury News

item.title: Caitlin Clark teammate's Kelsey Mitchell talks handling increased popularity
item.description: Indiana Fever guard Kelsey Mitchell talked about playing with the extra pairs of eyeballs on the WNBA, which were brought by Caitlin Clark.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: George heading to 76ers, Caldwell-Pope to Magic, CP3 to Spurs, AP sources say
item.description: Paul George is going back to the Eastern Conference, and surely has made Joel Embiid very happy in the process. George will sign a four-year, $212 million contract with the Philadelphia 76ers, a person with knowledge of the move told The Associated Press early Monday.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Paul George agrees to 4-year deal with 76ers: report
item.description: NBA star Paul George reportedly agreed to a four-year deal with the Philadelphia 76ers early Monday as free agency opened up. He spent five years with the Los Angeles Clippers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Broncos' Zach Wilson gets engaged to girlfriend in Italy: 'My best friend and my everything'
item.description: Denver Broncos quarterback Zach Wilson and his girlfriend Nicolette Dellanno announced Sunday they got engaged in Italy. It marks a wild offseason for the veteran.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Caitlin Clark's future is 'so bright,' WNBA legend Diana Taurasi says
item.description: Phoenix Mercury star Diana Taurasi praised Indiana Fever rookie Caitlin Clark after their game on Sunday, specifically talking about her resiliency.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Wimbledon starts with Carlos Alcaraz, Emma Raducanu and Coco Gauff on the schedule
item.description: WIMBLEDON, England (AP) — The 2024 edition of Wimbledon started on Monday, with defending champion Carlos Alcaraz and past Grand Slam title winners Coco Gauff and Emma Raducanu all set to play matches at Centre Court. Alcaraz just won his third major championship at the French Open three weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: All-Star Paul George set to join 76ers on a $212 million free-agent deal, AP source says
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Paul George and the Philadelphia 76ers have agreed to a four-year, $212 million free-agent contract, a person with knowledge of the deal said early Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rockies take final game against White Sox, Jackalopes tie in final game
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — It has been a week since the Grand Junction Jackalopes won a game. After losing the last five games to the Rocky Mountain Vibes, the Lopes looked to close out the series with a win.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Texas Rangers rookie Wyatt Langford becomes the 1st major leaguer to hit for the cycle in 2024
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Needing a home run for the cycle in the eighth inning, Wyatt Langford was focused on exactly that. Not that it changed his approach much. “I was obviously feeling really good at that point,” Langford said. “Just kind of one of those things that happens.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 7v7 Football: Torrey Pines 14, West Hills 0
item.description: In the "Battle on the Mountain" 7v7 Football Tournament at Ramona High School, Torrey Pines defeated West Hills 14-0.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: Toglia hits a sacrifice fly in the 14th inning as the Rockies top the White Sox 5-4
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Ryan McMahon scored on Michael Toglia's sacrifice fly in the 14th inning, and the Colorado Rockies stopped a five-game slide by topping the Chicago White Sox 5-4 on Sunday. McMahon began the 14th on second as the automatic runner. He advanced on Sam Hillard's sacrifice bunt.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: McLaughlin-Levrone sends pre-Olympic jolt running 50.65 to break world record (again) in 400 hurdles
item.description: Sydney McLaughlin-Levrone lowered her world record once again, running the 400-meter hurdles in 50.65 seconds Sunday to win the U.S. Olympic trials.
item.dc:creator: Eddie Pells, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: With England survival at stake, Jude Bellingham creates one of the great moments of Euro 2024
item.description: GELSENKIRCHEN, Germany (AP) — Jude Bellingham needed to produce something special to keep England at Euro 2024.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ROBSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Caitlin Clark and the Fever rally from 15 down to beat the Mercury 88-82
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Caitlin Clark had 15 points, 12 assists and nine rebounds and the Indiana Fever rallied for an 88-82 victory over the Phoenix Mercury on Sunday. Clark was just 4 of 14 from the field, and 2 of 10 from 3-point range.
item.dc:creator: JACK THOMPSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Warriors poised to take a step back as free agency opens
item.description: Chris Paul is gone and Klay Thompson is likely to leave as well, possibly with nothing in return for the Warriors.
item.dc:creator: Danny Emerman, The Mercury News

item.title: Brewers have American Family Field escalators inspected after malfunction results in 11 injuries
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — A spokesman for the Milwaukee Brewers said Sunday the team “was in the process of reaching out” to the 11 people injured after an American Family Field escalator malfunctioned.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Warriors waive Chris Paul: reports
item.description: (KRON) – The Golden State Warriors have waived veteran Chris Paul on Sunday, making him a free agent, according to multiple reports. The guard had a $30 million guarantee deal with the Warriors. Now that Paul is a free agent, he has reportedly signed with another team.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, KRON4

item.title: Tampa Bay Lightning acquire the rights to Jake Guentzel on the eve of free agency
item.description: The Lightning sent a 2025 third-round draft pick to the Carolina Hurricanes. The move allows the Lightning to sign Guentzel before he hits the open market on Monday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Utah Royals fire head coach Amy Rodriguez
item.description: In a move that was surprising to many, Utah Royals FC announced Sunday morning that they have fired head coach Amy Rodriguez.
item.dc:creator: Stockton Mair, ABC 4

item.title: 11 injured after escalator accelerates at Milwaukee Brewers ballpark
item.description: The escalator's malfunction resulted “in an increased downward speed,” the Brewers said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rosen, NewsNation

item.title: Sources: Bulls, former 1st-rounder Patrick Williams agree on 5-year, $90M deal
item.description: CHICAGO — The Bulls and former first-round draft pick Patrick Williams have reportedly agreed to a five-year, $90 million contract, thus avoiding free agency for the 22-year-old forward. Shams Charania of The Athletic first reported the deal on X late Saturday night, citing league sources.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: NFL violates antitrust law, Nuggets eye Klay Thompson
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — An eight-person jury ruled in favor of the plaintiffs, ordering the league to pay $14.39 billion in damages. This case centers on the offering of out-of-market Sunday games through a premium subscription service.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Ahead of the game against the Houston Astros, Mets fans cheer on their team
item.description: FLUSHING, Queens (PIX11) — The Mets winning streak came to an end Saturday after facing a tough loss against the Houston Astros. PIX11 News caught up with energized fans at a block party, before the game, who were hopeful for another win.
item.dc:creator: Zhané Caldwell, PIX 11

item.title: Utah HC takes nine players on second day of NHL Draft
item.description: LAS VEGAS (ABC4 Sports) - After selecting Tij Iginla and Cole Beaudoin in the first round of the NHL Draft on Friday, the Utah Hockey Club made nine more selections in the second round Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Stamkos plans to test free agency, Lightning GM says
item.description: Tampa Bay Lightning General Manager Julien BriseBois said on Saturday that Steven Stamkos plans to test the free agent market on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Little League: San Carlos 16, Serra Mesa 3
item.description: San Carlos Little League advances to the Semi-Finals of District 33's Winners Bracket, after defeating Serra Mesa 16-3.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: LeBron James to opt out of contract, but plans to return to Los Angeles Lakers: report
item.description: LeBron James will opt out of his player option for next season with the intention of working out a new contract with the Los Angeles Lakers, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Lightning's Steven Stamkos likely to hit free agency market, reports say
item.description: Tampa Bay Lightning captain Steven Stamkos is likely to hit the free agency market on Monday, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NHL Draft: San Jose Sharks' No. 1 overall selection has Warriors ties
item.description: (KRON) -- The San Jose Sharks selected forward Macklin Celebrini out of Boston University with the No. 1 overall pick of the NHL Draft on Friday. Celebrini is the son of Golden State Warriors VP of Player Health and Performance Rick Celebrini.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Luke Combs takes the wheel at Ally 400 NASCAR race in Nashville
item.description: Country music superstar Luke Combs will swap his guitar for driving gloves as the honorary pace car driver at the Ally 400 NASCAR Cup Series event on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Petrulis, NewsNation

item.title: Jury orders NFL to pay nearly $4.8B in 'Sunday Ticket' case
item.description: The lawsuit claimed the league broke antitrust laws by selling its package of Sunday games at an inflated price.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Athlete of the Week: FDU long jumper Salif Mane
item.description: TEANECK, N.J. (PIX11) -- For this week's New York Very Own, an alumnus returns as we feature Fairleigh Dickinson University FDU long jumper Salif Mane. Last year, we highlighted Salif's fantastic junior season for the Knights, but the jumper's career has only reached new lengths as a senior.
item.dc:creator: Perry Sook, PIX 11

item.title: Moose on the Loose: Torres Troubles
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- The Yankees are taking on water. They have lost four straight series. Their offense outside of Aaron Judge and Juan Soto looks very meek, and their starting rotation, which has been a strength, is now a weakness.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: Like father, like son: Lakers select Bronny James in NBA Draft
item.description: The Los Angeles Lakers have selected Bronny James with the 55th overall pick in the 2024 NBA Draft, raising the likelihood that the former USC Trojan will take the court with his father, future Hall-of-Famer LeBron James. Bronny, aka LeBron James Jr.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Jury orders NFL to pay nearly $4.8 billion in 'Sunday Ticket' case for violating antitrust laws
item.description: A jury in U.S. District Court ordered the NFL to pay nearly $4.8 billion in damages Thursday after ruling that the league violated antitrust laws in distributing out-of-market Sunday afternoon games on a premium subscription service.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: FIFA World Cup 26 will generate more than half a billion dollars for Los Angeles: study
item.description: The storylines in store for soccer fans ahead of FIFA World Cup 26 are already sizzling — a potential retirement sendoff for legends like Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo, an expanded field of 48 teams and likely the most talented team in United States Men's National Team history — but the tournam
item.dc:creator: KTLA 5
